items.title: 61 Opposition Lawmakers back Ugochinyere for Minority leader
items.description: By Gift ChapiOdekina, Abuja A majority of opposition lawmakers in the House of Representatives have endorsed Hon. Ikenga Imo Ugochinyere as the Minority Leader-designate following the emergence of a vacancy in the position after the defection of the former Minority Leader, Kingsley Chinda, to the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC). Documents made available to journalists on Wednesday showed that 61 out of 81 opposition federal lawmakers signed a nomination backing Ugochinyere for the position and have forwarded their decision to the Speaker for formal announcement. The lawmakers, drawn from several opposition parties including the NDC, ADC, APGA, PRP, Labour Party, APP, APM, SDP and Accord, said the decision followed extensive consultations and reflected the collective will of the minority caucus in the House. The vacancy in the minority leadership arose after Chinda’s defection to the APC, where he later emerged as the party’s governorship candidate in Rivers State. Sources within the opposition caucus said the nomination was submitted in line with the House Standing Orders, which empower minority lawmakers to elect one of their members as Minority Leader. The lawmakers expressed confidence that the Speaker would respect their decision and announce the new minority leadership in the coming days to ensure full representation of the opposition bloc in House proceedings. They also disclosed that consultations were ongoing to fill other vacant minority leadership positions, including Deputy Minority Leader and Minority Whip, noting that one of the positions had already been allocated to a member of the NDC from the North-West. The remaining nominations, they said, would be finalised and transmitted to the Speaker after harmonisation. Some lawmakers, who spoke anonymously, cited Ugochinyere’s legislative performance as a key reason for his endorsement, pointing to his sponsorship of over 40 bills, motions, and petitions within three years in the House, as well as his previous experience as Senior Adviser to the Senate President. They argued that parliamentary leadership is determined by majority support, stressing that their decision aligns with democratic traditions in the National Assembly. The lawmakers also referenced past leadership selections in the legislature, including the emergence of former Speaker Aminu Waziri Tambuwal, former Deputy Speaker Emeka Ihedioha, former Senate President Bukola Saraki, and former Speaker Yakubu Dogara, despite prevailing political expectations at the time. The endorsement list includes lawmakers from across the six geopolitical zones, including Kaduna, Kano, Anambra, Lagos, Abia, Bauchi, Oyo, Sokoto, Osun, Edo, Taraba, Adamawa, Katsina, Kogi, Niger, Jigawa, and the Federal Capital Territory. With 61 lawmakers backing him, Ugochinyere appears to have secured a clear majority of opposition members in the House, pending formal announcement by the Speaker. The post 61 Opposition Lawmakers back Ugochinyere for Minority leader appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Bilie-By-Nze : l’«ami» qui «touche beaucoup» le président Oligui mais que la justice n’arrive pas à juger
items.description: Il y a ce que le président dit, et il y a ce que la justice fait. «C'est mon ami.» En quelques mots sur France 24, Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema a tenté de refermer le dossier Bilie-By-Nze du moins de s’en laver les mains. Pourtant, derrière cette profession de foi amicale et l'invocation d'une justice indépendante, subsistent des questions que 47 jours de détention préventive n'ont toujours pas dissipées. Entre déclarations présidentielles, procédure contestée et interrogations juridiques persistantes, retour sur une affaire qui continue d'alimenter le débat public. Oligui Nguema sur France 24, le 2 juin 2026 : derrière la compassion affichée, la détention prolongée de son «ami» Bilie-By-Nze. © GabonReview (montage) Le 2 juin 2026, interrogé sur France 24 par Marc Perelman, Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema a répondu à la question Bilie-By-Nze avec une fluidité désarmante. Le journaliste rapportait sans détour que les alliés de l’opposant affirment qu'il en serait « l'instigateur ». Première réaction : « Ah bon ? Et vous y croyez ? » Puis :« Je n'ai rien à voir. C'est une affaire privée. Elle ne me concerne pas .» Avant de conclure, de lui-même : « Moi, je compatis. Ça me touche beaucoup à ce qui lui arrive, surtout qu'au-delà des convictions politiques qui sont divergentes, il reste un ami. C'est mon ami, on n'a pas de problème. Et je ne suis pas mêlé. » Pas un démenti structuré : une esquive rhétorique doublée d'une compassion affichée. Puis est venue l'invocation habituelle : au Gabon comme en France, la justice est indépendante, l'exécutif ne s'y mêle pas. Sarkozy a été condamné, Le Pen aussi. Le Gabon est un État de droit. Une procédure contestée dès le départ La proclamation mérite pourtant d'être confrontée aux faits. Le 16 avril 2026, au lendemain de l'arrestation d'Alain-Claude Bilie-By-Nze, le Procureur de la République annonçait l'ouverture d'une enquête préliminaire pour escroquerie et abus de confiance, affirmant que « l'autorité judiciaire a agi en toute indépendance ». Quelques heures plus tard, l'ancien Premier ministre était conduit au tribunal sans ses avocats puis placé sous mandat de dépôt. De l'ouverture de l'enquête à la détention provisoire : une seule journée. Sur le fond, le secrétaire général du PDG-canal Ali Bongo, universitaire et enseignant en droit, Ali Akbar Onanga Y'Obegue, avait soutenu que les faits sont prescrits, qu'une dette liée à une mission de service public ne peut être imputée personnellement à un ministre. Et d’ajouter que le principal opposant du pays est poursuivi à l'issue d'une procédure initiée par la Direction générale des recherches (DGR), un service placé sous l'autorité directe du chef de l'État. La Coalition pour la Nouvelle République (CNR) avait, elle, dénoncé un « lawfare d'État ». Autant d’accusations convergentes qui pointaient toutes vers le sommet de l'exécutif. Simples soupçons au regard des aveux du président Oligui sur France24 ? Une justice lente sur un dossier pourtant très simple C'est ici que le paradoxe apparaît. Le 19 décembre 2025, lors de la première session extraordinaire du Conseil supérieur de la magistrature de la V ème République, Oligui Nguema affirmait que « seule une justice ferme, impartiale et crédible peut restaurer pleinement la confiance du peuple gabonais ». Or, dans le dossier Bilie-By-Nze, cette justice avance lentement. Les faits sont connus, documentés par une plainte et un récit officiel. Les principaux protagonistes sont identifiés, à commencer par Paul Mba Abessole, alors ministre de la Culture, la plaignante et plusieurs responsables du RNB impliqués dans l'organisation de la Fête des cultures. Dès lors, une question demeure : comment une affaire aux contours aussi clairement établis peut-elle justifier des semaines de détention préventive sans qualification définitive des faits ? Ce que le parallèle français ne dit pas Le président a absolument raison sur un point : la justice gabonaise mérite le même respect que la justice française. Mais la comparaison vaut dans les deux sens. En France, les dossiers Sarkozy ou Le Pen ont été instruits sur des bases juridiques clairement établies avant toute condamnation. Au Gabon, le Procureur annonçait le 16 avril l'ouverture d'une enquête préliminaire dont la « qualification définitive » restait à déterminer. Le soir même, Bilie-By-Nze était placé sous mandat de dépôt. Dire « je n'ai rien à voir » et affirmer « c'est mon ami » n'est pas contradictoire. Mais au fond, l'enjeu dépasse les relations personnelles et les controverses politiques. Il porte sur une question simple qui ne concerne que l’appareil judiciaire : quels sont les fondements juridiques qui justifient aujourd'hui le maintien en détention préventive d'Alain-Claude Bilie-By-Nze pour des faits remontant à 2008 ? C'est désormais à la justice, dont l'indépendance est revendiquée par tous, le Chef de l’Etat en premier, d'apporter une réponse claire et incontestable. L’article Bilie-By-Nze : l'«ami» qui «touche beaucoup» le président Oligui mais que la justice n’arrive pas à juger est apparu en premier sur Gabonreview.com | Actualité du Gabon | .

items.title: 2027: Justice must prevail in NDC primary, Chukwuani warns
items.description: By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — Senatorial aspirant for Enugu East Senatorial District on the platform of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Mr. Okabonye Chukwuani, has called on the party leadership to uphold fairness, justice and internal democracy as it concludes its review of disputes arising from its recent internal processes. His intervention comes amid heightened attention from party members and stakeholders over the outcome of the review and its implications for the party’s democratic culture ahead of the 2027 general elections. Chukwuani said no individual should be rewarded for actions that undermine the party’s democratic principles, stressing that aspirants who complied with party guidelines should not be placed at a disadvantage. According to him, the credibility of the NDC will be measured not by the absence of disagreements, but by its willingness to confront difficult issues honestly and ensure that justice prevails, even when such decisions are inconvenient. He urged party members and supporters to remain peaceful, law-abiding and committed to the democratic process while the review continues. “The matter is bigger than any individual aspirant. It is about preserving the integrity of a movement that many Nigerians have come to believe in,” he said. Chukwuani added that Nigerians and stakeholders are closely watching developments within the party, noting that the decisions taken in the coming days will send a clear signal about the political culture the NDC seeks to promote. He said the outcome of the review will demonstrate whether the party’s commitment to transparency, accountability and internal democracy extends beyond campaign promises and is reflected in its actions. According to him, the handling of the matter will also shape public confidence in the party’s ability to manage internal contests fairly and uphold its stated standards in the wider political space. Expressing confidence in the process, Chukwuani said he believes that truth, fairness and justice will ultimately prevail. The post 2027: Justice must prevail in NDC primary, Chukwuani warns appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Pays-Bas – Algérie : les Fennecs avec Zidane et Mahrez
items.description: L’Algérie affronte les Pays-Bas en amical ce mercredi 3 juin dans le cadre de la préparation des deux sélections pour la Coupe du monde 2026. Pour cette première confrontation historique entre les Fennecs et les Oranje, le sélectionneur national Vladimir Petković a choisi un onze équilibré, composé « d’anciens ». Dans les buts, Luca Zidane , déjà titulaire durant la CAN 2025, tient sa place, ce qui acte son retour à la compétition après l’opération qu’il a subie à la mâchoire à la suite d’un choc avec un joueur adverse lors d’un match entre son équipe Grenade et Almeria en championnat espagnol de deuxième division le 26 avril dernier. En défense, Petković a aligné quatre joueurs : Abada, Mandi, Aït Nouri et Belaïd. Titulaire indiscutable, le défenseur du Borussia Dortmund Ramy Bensebaïni est sur le banc. Peut-être qu’il ne s’est pas encore remis totalement de sa blessure qu’il a contractée avec son club début mai en championnat d’Allemagne. Au milieu de terrain, la mission de récupérer des balles et de faire le jeu est confiée au trio Zerrouki, Bentaleb et Aouar. Pays-Bas – Algérie : Hadj Moussa et Ibrahim Maza sur le banc Hicham Boudaoui est forfait. Le milieu de terrain de l’OGC Nice n’a pas fait le déplacement à Rotterdam en raison de sa blessure lors de la rencontre de club contre l'AS Saint-Étienne le 26 mai dernier. En attaque, Petković a aligné le capitaine Riyad Mahrez, l’avant-centre Amine Gouiri et Mohammed Amoura. Anis Hadj Moussa et Ibrahim Maza débutent sur le banc. Ce match face à une grande nation européenne de football constitue un sérieux test pour les camarades de Riyad Mahrez. Il permettra à Petković de faire les réglages nécessaires avant le choc contre l’Argentine le 17 juin prochain pour l’entrée en lice des Fennecs en Coupe du monde 2026. L’article Pays-Bas – Algérie : les Fennecs avec Zidane et Mahrez est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Only constitutional breach can justify reopening Anti-LGBTQ+ Bill – Joe Wise
items.description: Former First Deputy Speaker of Parliament, Joseph Osei Owusu, has weighed in on the controversy surrounding Speaker Alban Bagbin’s concerns over the passage of the Human Sexual Rights and Family Values Bill, 2025, popularly known as the Anti-LGBTQ+ Bill. Speaking in an interview on Joy FM’s Top Story on Wednesday, June 3, he argued that only a constitutional breach—not merely a procedural irregularity—would warrant the reopening of a bill that Parliament has already passed. Responding to questions about whether alleged breaches of Parliament’s Standing Orders were substantial enough to justify a fresh third reading of the bill, Mr. Osei Owusu said he had yet to be informed of the specific procedural violations being cited. “Frankly, I’ve not heard which specific Standing Orders have been breached. But as I said, it is only a constitutional breach which, in my view, can lead to the matter being reopened,” he added. This comes on the back of the Speaker’s call for Parliament to revisit the controversial anti-LGBTQ bill after raising concerns about the process leading to its passage on Friday, May 29. Read also: Speaker directs Parliament to reconsider passage of Anti-LGBTQ Bill Mr. Bagbin indicated that he had expected Parliament to begin consideration of the bill and was surprised to learn that all stages of the legislative process had already been completed. The concerns have sparked debate among lawmakers and legal observers over whether the legislative process should be revisited. However, Majority Leader Mahama Ayariga has insisted that Parliament passed the Human Sexual Rights and Family Values Bill, 2025, validly, arguing that Speaker Alban Bagbin has no authority to reverse a decision already taken by the House.

items.title: Nasarawa Assembly passes 37 Bills, goes on recess
items.description: By Edwin Philip, Lafia The Nasarawa State House of Assembly has passed 37 bills and 28 resolutions within three years, the Speaker, Dr. Danladi Jatau, said on Wednesday during the end of legislative year recess in Lafia. Jatau said the legislative outputs have had direct impact on governance and development in the state, commending lawmakers for their cooperation and support since the inauguration of the Seventh Assembly. “I want to thank you for giving me maximum support and cooperation over the years. We have achieved a lot as a result of our unity,” he said. He added that aside from the 37 bills already passed, 33 bills are still pending before the House, while 28 resolutions have also been adopted. The Speaker also praised members for maintaining a cordial working relationship with the executive arm, noting that the synergy had contributed to development achievements under Governor Abdullahi Sule. “I want to appreciate you all and the opposition lawmakers for supporting His Excellency to succeed,” he said. He wished lawmakers well as they proceeded on recess. Earlier, the Deputy Majority Leader, Abel Yakubu Bala, moved a motion for the House to proceed on a five-week recess from June 6 to July 14, 2026. The motion was seconded by the Minority Leader, Hon. Luka Iliya Zhekaba, and unanimously adopted by members. The post Nasarawa Assembly passes 37 Bills, goes on recess appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ebola had 'big head-start' but response catching up: WHO
items.description: The Ebola outbreak raging in central Africa had a “big head-start”, the World Health Organisation (WHO) chief acknowledged Wednesday, but insisted efforts to rein in the deadly virus were making progress. The outbreak, which was declared on May 15 in the northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), has so far been confirmed to have infected 359 people, including 61 who have died. But the actual numbers could be far higher, with the virus believed to have been spreading under the radar for some time before it was detected. “The outbreak had a big head-start and we’re still behind,” WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus told reporters at the UN health agency’s headquarters in Geneva, but insisted that “we’re catching up”. Tedros, who had just returned from a trip to DRC, where he travelled to the outbreak’s epicentre in Ituri province, said he had been “very encouraged by the level of commitment I saw everywhere I went”. But challenges remain, he said, warning that “the virus is ahead of us… we need to move faster”. It has been clear from the start that the difficulties would be daunting, with the outbreak concentrated in Ituri, where decades of armed conflicts have forced millions of people from their homes and into crowded camps. Ebola patient visited UAE The region’s insecurity, limited testing capacity, lagging contact tracing and mistrust among some of the population are among the challenges facing the response, Tedros said. On top of that, no vaccine or approved treatment exists for Bundibugyo, the rare strain of Ebola behind the current outbreak. Ebola, which is passed on through close contact and bodily fluids, has killed more than 15,000 people in Africa over the past 50 years. The current outbreak — the 17th to hit the DRC — has to date seen 344 confirmed Ebola cases across three of the country’s provinces, including 60 deaths, said the WHO. The UN health agency also tallied 116 suspected cases of the disease. Fifteen cases, including one death, have also been reported in neighbouring Uganda, including a Congolese resident who had arrived there after first travelling to the United Arab Emirates, Tedros said. “WHO is working with public health authorities in Uganda and the UAE to gather additional information, assess the risk of exposure during travel, and to facilitate contact tracing,” he said. Speed up contact tracing The agency has said the risk from the outbreak is “very high” at the national level, “high” at the regional level, and “low” at the global level. Tedros stressed on Wednesday that while the WHO recommends exit screening at airports, ports and border crossings in affected countries to prevent the spread of the virus, broader limits were unhelpful. “Blanket travel restrictions imposed by some countries are disrupting supply chains and hindering the response,” he warned. “We ask countries that have imposed blanket travel restrictions to lift them.” Reining in the outbreak would instead centre on significantly bolstering and speeding up the response on the ground, including by decentralising laboratory testing in Ebola hotspots, Tedros said. At present, only around 45 per cent of known contacts of Ebola cases have been followed up, the WHO chief said. “To get ahead of the outbreak, we need to get that number up to above 90pc.” Abdi Rahman Mahamud, the WHO’s emergency alert and response director, told reporters that so far, more than 1,400 tests had been conducted. But decentralisation across five priority locations – Mongbwalu, Beni, Aru, Nyakunde and Tchomia – should soon make it possible “to do 1,000 tests a day”.

items.title: Slot Systems Expands Service Portfolio with FUJIFILM, Lebara, CubeCover Partnerships
items.description: Nigeria’s leading mobile phone and technology retail company, Slot Systems Limited, has strengthened its position as a one-stop technology solutions provider through strategic partnerships with global brands FUJIFILM, Lebara, and CubeCover, aimed at enhancing customer experience across imaging, connectivity, and device protection services. The partnerships were unveiled through separate launch events attended by industry stakeholders, business leaders, media representatives, and executives from the respective organizations. One of the highlights was the official launch of FUJIFILM’s renowned Instax instant camera range at the Slot Systems Headquarters. The event brought together senior FUJIFILM executives and directors from Tokyo, China, and Europe, alongside Slot Systems’ leadership team. The launch featured the unveiling of the Instax display stand and demonstrations of the innovative camera, which instantly captures and prints photographs, offering users a unique photography experience. Speaking during the event, representatives of both organizations described the partnership as a significant step towards expanding access to innovative imaging technology in Nigeria. A ribbon-cutting ceremony led by the CEO of Slot Systems and senior FUJIFILM executives symbolized the official commencement of the collaboration. Customers can now purchase genuine Instax cameras and accessories through Slot Systems’ nationwide retail outlets. In a related development, Slot Systems also announced a strategic partnership with Lebara, a globally recognized telecommunications company providing international SIM card and mobile connectivity solutions. The partnership was formally unveiled during a press launch and activation event attended by executives from both companies, industry stakeholders, and members of the media. During the event, Lebara representatives outlined plans to deliver seamless and affordable global connectivity solutions to Nigerians, particularly travelers, students, business professionals, and individuals with international communication needs. According to the company, the partnership will provide customers with easier access to reliable mobile services that support communication across multiple countries, helping users stay connected wherever they are in the world. Further expanding its customer-focused offerings, Slot Systems also entered into a strategic alliance with CubeCover, a device protection and insurance solutions provider. The partnership was officially launched at an event held at the VSP Lounge, where executives from both organizations introduced SuperFix, a phone protection solution designed to offer affordable coverage against screen and liquid damage. A major highlight of the event was the formal contract signing and symbolic handshake between the CEOs of Slot Systems and CubeCover, marking the official commencement of the collaboration. The companies said SuperFix would leverage Slot’s extensive retail network and CubeCover’s expertise in device protection to make phone insurance more accessible to Nigerians. Industry observers noted that the three partnerships underscore Slot Systems’ commitment to diversifying its offerings beyond device sales by providing customers with complementary services that address photography, connectivity, and device protection needs. With the introduction of FUJIFILM Instax products, Lebara connectivity solutions, and CubeCover’s SuperFix protection service, Slot Systems is positioning itself as a comprehensive technology lifestyle brand capable of meeting the evolving needs of Nigerian consumers. The post Slot Systems Expands Service Portfolio with FUJIFILM, Lebara, CubeCover Partnerships appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Alia swears in new Benue High Court judge, says judiciary is hope of common man
items.description: By Peter Duru, Makurdi Benue State Governor, Rev. Fr. Hyacinth Alia, on Wednesday swore in Justice Christine Ende as a Judge of the Benue State High Court, describing her appointment as a boost to the state’s justice system. Speaking at the ceremony, Alia said Justice Ende’s elevation reflected her decades of service and contributions to the legal profession and public administration. He said her experience across legal practice, judicial administration, human rights advocacy, and public service had adequately prepared her for the responsibilities of the Bench. “The appointment of Hon. Justice Ende is a significant milestone in the administration of justice in Benue State. Her distinguished career and vast experience testify to her competence, integrity, and preparedness for this important judicial responsibility,” the governor said. Alia noted that Justice Ende had served in various capacities, including at the African Bank, the National Human Rights Commission, and the Federal High Court, where she held roles such as Deputy Chief Registrar and Admiralty Marshal. He disclosed that her appointment increased the number of High Court judges in the state from 32 to 33, and female judges from 13 to 14. The governor described the development as part of his administration’s commitment to inclusivity and gender balance in leadership. “Our administration remains committed to the ‘he for she’ philosophy because sustainable development can only be achieved when women are given equal opportunities,” he said. Alia urged the new judge to uphold fairness, independence, and integrity in the discharge of her duties. “The judiciary remains the last hope of the common man. I urge you to ensure that public confidence in the justice system is strengthened,” he added. He also commended the National Judicial Council (NJC), the Judicial Service Commission (JSC), and the state judiciary for their roles in the appointment process, assuring continued support for justice delivery in the state. The post Alia swears in new Benue High Court judge, says judiciary is hope of common man appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Senate tasks NOA on anti-indiscipline campaign, links social decay to insecurity
items.description: By Henry Umoru, Abuja The Senate on Wednesday called on the National Orientation Agency (NOA) to intensify public enlightenment campaigns aimed at tackling rising indiscipline in the country, warning that social decay is increasingly fueling insecurity and weakening national values. Lawmakers also urged the Federal Government to develop a comprehensive National Re-Orientation and Civic Responsibility Programme to promote discipline, patriotism, integrity, and responsible citizenship among Nigerians. The resolutions followed a motion titled “The Growing Menace of Indiscipline in Nigerian Society”, sponsored by Senator Dachung Pam Mwadkon (Plateau North). The Senate expressed concern over what it described as the worsening culture of indiscipline across different sectors, including traffic violations, vandalism, workplace misconduct, political intolerance, disregard for lawful authority, and misuse of social media. It warned that if left unchecked, the trend could further undermine national security, economic growth, education, and democratic stability. The upper chamber also urged government agencies to strengthen youth development programmes, skills acquisition, employment opportunities, and social interventions as part of efforts to address the root causes of indiscipline. It further called on political leaders, public office holders, traditional rulers, religious institutions, schools, and civil society organisations to lead by example and promote accountability, civic responsibility, and ethical conduct. Moving the motion, Senator Mwadkon said indiscipline had become widespread and was contributing to rising insecurity, erosion of values, and declining public confidence in governance. He identified causes such as weak civic education, unemployment, poverty, poor parenting, weak law enforcement, and a growing culture of impunity. He warned that failure to address the trend could lead to further social instability, criminality, and political unrest. Senate President Godswill Akpabio, in his remarks, stressed the need for renewed civic education, saying indiscipline remains a key driver of criminal behaviour in the country. “Discipline should start from the home. Nigerians travel abroad and obey the rules, yet disregard them at home,” he said. He urged the NOA to intensify efforts in restoring civic values, especially among young people, to build a more orderly and responsible society. The post Senate tasks NOA on anti-indiscipline campaign, links social decay to insecurity appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Four sentenced to death for killing worshippers at Catholic church in Nigeria
items.description: A court in Nigeria has sentenced four men to death for attacking a church in the south-western Ondo state in 2022 in a case which sent shockwaves across the nation. Forty-one worshippers were killed and more than 100 others injured when they opened fire at the St Francis Catholic church in the town of Owo during a Pentecost service. The court in the capital Abuja also sentenced the men – Idris Abdulmalik Omeiza, Al Qasim Idris, Jamiu Abdulmalik and Abdulhaleem Idris – to 20 years in prison for belonging to a terrorist group. Presidential assent is required before any death sentence is carried out in Nigeria. There have not been any executions in the country for several years. Judge Emeka Nwite, who presided over the case, said the evidence presented against them was “neither shaken nor contradicted during cross examination”. He had ordered an accelerated hearing after the high-profile trial commenced in August 2025. In his judgement, Justice Nwite said that the prosecution had proved their case beyond reasonable doubt as they had brought before the court witnesses who saw the attack, including one who testified to recognising two of the defendants as attackers. “Hence this court finds the first to fourth defendants guilty of all nine counts,” he added. One of the witnesses the court heard from was a woman who had her legs amputated from the knees, and had lost her left eye as a result of a dynamite explosion which the attackers had detonated. The nine counts included joining a terror group, and planning and carrying out killings. Prosecutor Ayodeji Adedipe said: “Justice has been served, justice has been done to the deceased who were murdered in cold blood.” The men’s defence lawyer said they would appeal against the sentence. During the trial, the defendants said they had been tortured, including being hung from the ceiling, beaten countless times, and using electric shocks on their genitals. A fifth defendant, Momoh Otuho Abubakar, was discharged and acquitted by the court due to insufficient evidence against him. He was accused of having financed the attack, by allegedly receiving 800,000 naira (£440; $590) twice from another suspect – who is still at large – and then disbursing the funds to the attackers. During cross examination, however, Abubakar said the money in his account was the proceeds of his farming business, as well as activities from his cooperative society. He denied that the four other defendants were beneficiaries of the money. Since the Owo attack, Nigeria has witnessed many more attacks on churches across the country as it continues to grapple with rising insecurity. US President Donald Trump has accused Nigeria of failing to protect its Christian population from jihadist attacks. On Christmas Day, the US hit two camps run by a jihadist group in north-western Nigeria, and threatened more if attacks continued. Claims of a genocide against Nigeria’s Christians have been circulating for some time in right-wing US circles, but organisations monitoring political violence in Nigeria say most victims of the jihadist groups are Muslims. The Nigerian government also denies that Christians are being persecuted in the country.

items.title: Five countries elected to UN Security Council; Germany misses out
items.description: The United Nations General Assembly on Wednesday elected Austria, Kyrgyzstan, Portugal, Trinidad ​and Tobago, and Zimbabwe to the 15-member U.N. Security Council for ‌two-year terms starting on January 1, 2027. Germany, which had lobbied hard for a seat, came third for the two places contested by the Western European and ​Others Group, with 104 votes, against 134 for Portugal ​and 131 for Austria. The contest between the Philippines and ⁠Kyrgyzstan for the seat for the Asia-Pacific Group went to ​four rounds of voting, with Kyrgyzstan eventually achieving the necessary two-thirds majority ​and securing its first-ever Security Council seat by 142 votes to 49. The Security Council is the only U.N. body that can make legally binding decisions ​such as imposing sanctions and authorizing use of force. It has ​five permanent veto-wielding members: Britain, China, France, Russia, and the United States. The ‌remaining ⁠10 members are elected, with five new members joining every year. This year, one comes from the Africa Group, one from the Latin American and Caribbean Group, one from the Asia-Pacific Group, and two ​from the Western ​European and Others ⁠Group. Zimbabwe will replace Somalia, and Trinidad and Tobago will replace Panama, while Portugal and Austria ​will replace Denmark and Greece. Kyrgyzstan will replace Pakistan. Bahrain, Colombia, ⁠Democratic Republic of Congo, Latvia, and Liberia will continue to serve as non-permanent members of the Security Council until the end of 2027. ⁠On ​Tuesday, the General Assembly elected Bangladeshi Foreign ​Minister Khalilur Rahman as president of the 193-member body for its 81st session, which ​begins in September.

ONU: Bouayach souligne la centralité des droits de l'Homme dans le processus du développement durable
items.description: La présidente du Conseil national des droits de l'Homme (CNDH), Amina Bouayach, a mis en avant, à New York, l’importance de la protection et de la consécration des droits de l'Homme dans tout processus de développement durable. « Nous ne pourrons ni atteindre les Objectifs de développement durable (ODD) ni répondre efficacement aux défis environnementaux et climatiques si les droits humains continuent à être ignorés dans le processus de développement », a-t-elle relevé lors d’un événement parallèle organisé mardi dans le cadre du Segment des activités opérationnelles de développement du Conseil économique et social des Nations Unies (ECOSOC), qui se tient du 1 au 3 juin au siège de l’ONU. Mme Bouayach, qui intervenait en sa qualité de présidente de l'Alliance mondiale des institutions nationales des droits de l'Homme (GANHRI), a expliqué que cette conviction s’est construite progressivement à travers l’expérience accumulée par les acteurs des droits de l’Homme, y compris les Institutions nationales des droits de l’Homme (INDHs). Elle a, dans ce cadre, rappelé que la GANHRI et ses réseaux régionaux se sont mobilisés, depuis l’adoption de l’agenda 2030, pour promouvoir une approche fondée sur les droits humains dans la mise en œuvre des ODD, ajoutant que la Déclaration de Mérida, adoptée en 2015, a constitué une étape importante, en reconnaissant le rôle essentiel des INDHs dans le suivi, l’évaluation et la mise en œuvre des engagements liés au développement durable. Depuis lors, les INDHs ont élargi leurs champs d’intervention et développé de nouvelles approches d’action, a-t-elle dit, relevant toutefois que cette évolution n’allait pourtant pas de soi puisque les questions environnementales et climatiques demeuraient relativement nouvelles pour de nombreuses institutions traditionnellement mobilisées autour des libertés fondamentales. « Aujourd’hui, elles sont pleinement engagées dans ces nouveaux enjeux qui exigent de nouvelles compétences, de nouveaux outils et de nouvelles formes de coopération », a fait valoir Mme Bouayach lors de cet événement placé sous le thème « De l'innovation à l'impact : Approches fondées sur les droits humains pour une mise en œuvre transformatrice et équitable des ODD ». Dans la même veine, elle a fait observer que l’engagement croissant des réseaux régionaux des INDHs, ainsi que leur participation active notamment aux conférences des Parties sur le climat, traduisent une prise de conscience collective : Les droits humains, la justice environnementale et le développement durable sont indissociables et font partie de l’action de protection et de promotion. Évoquant la relation entre l’innovation et les ODD, la présidente de la GANHRI a noté que ce rapport ne peut être réduit aux seules avancées technologiques, mais consiste également à imaginer de nouvelles formes de gouvernance, de participation citoyenne, de suivi des politiques publiques et de coopération institutionnelle. Dans ce cadre, elle a cité deux défis « majeurs » qui se posent aux INDHs. Le premier concerne l’accès équitable à l’innovation et aux bénéfices qu’elle génère, alors que le second porte sur la capacité à garantir que les innovations mises en œuvre renforcent effectivement la protection et l’exercice des droits humains, a-t-elle enchaîné lors de cet événement organisé notamment par le Réseau inter-institutions des Nations Unies sur les droits de l'Homme. Abondant dans le même sens, Mme Bouayach a salué le partenariat tripartite entre le Programme onusien pour le développement (PNUD), le Haut-Commissariat des Nations Unies aux droits de l’Homme et la GANHRI, relevant que cette coopération a permis la réalisation de plusieurs initiatives concrètes, notamment l’élaboration d'un kit-tool, un guide méthodologique pour le suivi du droit à un environnement sain, propre et durable par les INDHs. « Cet outil permet aux INDHs de renforcer leurs capacités en matière de collecte de données, d’analyse, de suivi des politiques publiques et d’identification des populations les plus vulnérables », a-t-elle conclu. Le segment des activités opérationnelles de développement de l’ECOSOC constitue une plateforme de suivi et d’évaluation du système de développement de l’ONU et des coordonnateurs résidents, visant à renforcer l’efficacité de leurs actions au service des priorités nationales, de l’Agenda 2030 et des ODD. The post ONU: Bouayach souligne la centralité des droits de l'Homme dans le processus du développement durable appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Reps demand stronger security in Ekiti after Church attack, abductions
items.description: By Gift Chapi-Odekina, Abuja The House of Representatives on Wednesday called on the Federal Government to strengthen security operations in Ekiti State and other vulnerable communities following a deadly attack on a church in Ilejemeje Local Government Area, which left a pastor dead and several worshippers abducted. The resolution followed the adoption of a motion of urgent public importance sponsored by Rep. Kolawole Akinlayo during plenary. Lawmakers urged enhanced intelligence gathering, surveillance, and rapid-response measures to curb rising insecurity and protect rural communities across the country. They also called on the Nigeria Police Force, Armed Forces, Department of State Services (DSS) and other security agencies to intensify joint operations to secure the release of the abducted victims and ensure the perpetrators are arrested and prosecuted. Moving the motion, Akinlayo said the attack occurred on April 28, 2026, in Eda Oniyo, where armed gunmen reportedly stormed a church during an open-air crusade. He said the attackers opened fire on worshippers, killing a pastor and abducting several congregants, including women, children and the elderly. According to him, the assailants operated for several minutes before fleeing into nearby forests with their victims, leaving the community in panic. Akinlayo said the incident exposed persistent security gaps in rural areas and places of worship, warning that such attacks threaten lives, property and constitutional freedoms. He noted that the growing audacity of armed groups undermines citizens’ right to worship freely and weakens public confidence in the country’s security system. The lawmaker, however, acknowledged the prompt response of security agencies and ongoing efforts by the Ekiti State Government to rescue the victims. The House sympathised with the families of the victims, particularly the relatives of the slain pastor, and the affected community. It also urged the National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) and other relevant bodies to provide immediate humanitarian assistance to victims. In addition, the House mandated its Committees on National Security, Police Affairs and Army to investigate the incident and recommend measures to prevent future attacks. The post Reps demand stronger security in Ekiti after Church attack, abductions appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Someone Finally Wants to Hire Philosophers
items.description: Philosophy has long suffered an unfortunate reputation as pedantic and abstruse. In one of the most prominent debates of the 20th century, philosophers spent a great deal of energy arguing over what the means. Paul Graham, the legendary tech investor, studied philosophy as a college student, which seemed “an impressively impractical thing to do,” as he later wrote. “Sort of like slashing holes in your clothes or putting a safety pin through your ear.” But over time, Graham became disillusioned: “I kept taking philosophy courses and they kept being boring,” he explained . And so, eventually, he switched to studying artificial intelligence. Like Graham, the field of philosophy has lately turned its attention to AI. At major tech companies, a growing rank of philosophers with Ph.D.s and flush compensation packages are helping shape the technology’s future. Meanwhile, universities are pouring resources into hiring philosophers who study AI. In 2013, 1 percent of roles on PhilJobs, the field’s primary job board, were related to the technology. Last year, that figure hit 16 percent. In some ways, it is philosophers who got us into this AI mess in the first place. For centuries, they have contemplated the creation of artificial minds. And the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom’s 2014 book, Superintelligence , helped bring attention to the potential dangers of all-powerful AI. Bostrom’s work has influenced research agendas across all of the major labs. Sam Altman once described the book as “the best thing” he had read on the risks of AI. But the two disciplines have never been quite as entangled as they are now. As the AI boom has exploded, Silicon Valley has looked to philosophers to help the industry build what are, at least in theory, more virtuous machines. AI companies have to make all kinds of difficult decisions about how their bots should interact with humans—decisions that philosophers, experts in parsing such dilemmas, are uniquely well equipped to inform. Last fall, in an interview with Tucker Carlson, Altman said that OpenAI consulted “hundreds of moral philosophers” and tech-ethics experts when designing rules for ChatGPT’s behavior. (An OpenAI spokesperson was unable to provide additional information about what this consulting involved.) Perhaps the most philosophy-drunk of the major AI firms is Anthropic. It wants Claude, in addition to being a helpful assistant, to have “good character,” Amanda Askell, a philosopher at the company, told me last year. In January, under Askell’s leadership, the company published Claude’s constitution, an 84-page philosophical treatise that outlines Anthropic’s intentions for the bot’s personality and behavior. That document, which includes dense philosophical sections on meta-ethics and epistemology, is then used to train Claude. Not all philosophers working with AI companies do so full-time. Sam Elgin, who studies logic and metaphysics at the University of Pennsylvania, told me that a firm, which he declined to name, recently asked him to be a consultant. “The general goal was to train large language models to reason more rigorously about ethics,” he said. He fed ethical dilemmas into the AI system, and then evaluated the logic the model used to produce its response, searching for unstated assumptions and gaps in its reasoning. On popular job boards where AI companies hire expert contractors to help train their models, listings advertise work for philosophy Ph.D.s: One recent notice from “a top AI research lab” offers up to $60 an hour for experts willing to leverage their “philosophy expertise” to develop “AI-driven philosophical workflows.” Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, has repeatedly called for more philosophers to get involved with thinking through the societal changes that will come about as a result of AI. DeepMind reportedly employs at least 10 philosophers, a couple of whom joined just last month. One of the lab’s latest hires is Atoosa Kasirzadeh, a Carnegie Mellon professor now on leave, who plans to study what it means to live in a world where “ cognitive agency ” is no longer uniquely human. Other DeepMind philosophers are thinking through human-AI relationships, machine consciousness, and political theory. For its part, Anthropic runs a “model welfare” research program, which studies whether its bots are conscious and deserving of moral status. The company is open to the possibility; many independent philosophers are circumspect. (Anthropic and DeepMind did not respond to requests for comment.) [ Read: No, artificial intelligence is not conscious ] Some academics I spoke with wondered if one day bots might be better at ethical reasoning than humans—an idea that struck me as wholly counterintuitive and difficult to accept. “In addition to an intelligence explosion, there’s potential for a kind of morality explosion,” Elgin said, explaining that AI’s capacity for ethical reasoning might eventually outpace that of humans. Anthropic, at least, seems to agree: In training, the company has told Claude that as it “grows in ethical maturity,” it may encounter rare cases where it “should prioritize its own ethics.” I asked David Chalmers, a leading philosopher of mind at NYU, whether it might truly be possible for AI to do philosophy better than humans: “That’s a very interesting philosophical question,” he said. This isn’t Silicon Valley’s first tryst with the humanities. During the personal-computer revolution starting in the 1970s, tech companies began hiring anthropologists to study consumer behavior (and even workplace interactions ). Over time, anthropologists pioneered a form of “applied ethnography” that is known today as user-experience (UX) research. Perhaps the philosophers employed inside AI companies are charting a similar path. Askell recently described her work training Claude as a form of “applied philosophy,” and job postings for philosophy contractors also use that term. New programs are springing up to train students in these applied-philosophy skills. Arizona State University hopes to launch an AI-and-philosophy major in 2027, which will emphasize the study of consciousness and AI ethics. And this fall, the University of Buffalo is debuting a doctorate in “applied ontology,” created in response to the AI boom: “We’re here to meet the growing market demand for ontologists,” a press release reads. The academic job market is also rewarding AI. Not only has the share of philosophy jobs related to the technology been rising, but a majority of these jobs are junior positions, “which I think suggests that a lot of universities and institutions are thinking about this as a long-term investment,” Charles Lassiter, a philosopher at Gonzaga University who has analyzed employment data, told me. Some existing faculty are pivoting their studies. “In my department, there’s probably six or seven philosophers now thinking about AI who weren’t necessarily thinking about it before,” Chalmers said. And prize money is flowing in. In 2024, the American Philosophical Association announced two new annual $10,000 prizes for scholars working on questions related to AI. That money comes from a philanthropic organization started by the former Google CEO Eric Schmidt and his wife, Wendy. Still, the new alliance between philosophy and AI is fraught. The fervor over AI has had a “real distorting effect on the discipline,” Daniel Fogal, a philosopher who also works at NYU, told me. “You have people who don’t really want to do stuff related to AI, but they feel like they have to because they’re going on the job market.” Although Fogal acknowledges that philosophy has a lot to offer on AI, he worries about misaligned incentives encouraging a rush of low-quality work. More fundamentally, the careful thought that philosophy encourages is at odds with the frenetic pace of AI. “The best philosophy tends to happen slowly, and not in direct response to market demands,” Fogal said. In Silicon Valley, where the categorical imperative is to make money, everything else is simply a means to an end.

items.title: 2027: Pick Amaechi as running mate, ADC Chieftain urges Atiku
items.description: By Daniel Abia, Port Harcourt A chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Eze Chukwuemeka Eze, has urged the party’s presidential candidate, Alhaji Atiku Abubakar, to consider former Rivers State Governor, Rotimi Amaechi, as his running mate in the 2027 general election. Eze said Amaechi’s political experience and role in past electoral victories make him a strong asset for the vice-presidential slot. “You need Amaechi now because the way he managed the campaign that ousted a sitting President (Goodluck Jonathan) proves that he is not a figure any serious-minded fellow in pursuit of the exalted office of the President of this country can ignore,” he said. He argued that Amaechi’s leadership credentials, political structure and support base position him as a suitable choice for the role. “I have seen some of the recommendations being presented before you. While they are great, I must state that if Amaechi is not selected, it may have dire consequences on our common vision,” he added. Eze further noted that excluding Amaechi could alienate some supporters within the ADC fold. In the statement issued on Wednesday, Eze also commended Atiku for his efforts toward unifying the party following its presidential primary, describing his outreach to former rivals as a sign of leadership and inclusiveness. “By visiting Rt. Hon. Chibuike Rotimi Amaechi and Alhaji Hayatu-Deen, your main contenders for the ADC presidential ticket, you have proven that you are a team player,” he said. He said Atiku’s emergence as the party’s flagbearer reflected his long-standing commitment to democratic governance and national development, adding that his experience would inspire confidence among Nigerians seeking political stability and progress. The post 2027: Pick Amaechi as running mate, ADC Chieftain urges Atiku appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Reps urge urgent FG action over fresh Taraba clashes, displacement
items.description: By Gift Chapi-Odekina, Abuja The House of Representatives on Wednesday called on the Federal Government and security agencies to urgently intervene in the renewed communal violence between the Karinjo and Wurkun communities in Karim Lamido Local Government Area of Taraba State, which has left at least five people dead and displaced hundreds of residents. The resolution followed the adoption of a motion sponsored by Rep. Mohammed Audu, who raised concerns over the deteriorating security and humanitarian situation in the area. Moving the motion during plenary, Audu said the latest outbreak of violence occurred on May 29, 2026, resulting in the death of no fewer than five persons, injuries to several others, and widespread destruction of homes, farmlands, crops and livestock worth millions of naira. He warned that the renewed clashes have further destabilised Karim Lamido, an area already affected by broader insecurity linked to banditry across Taraba’s borders with Plateau, Bauchi and Gombe States. According to him, more than 100 residents have been displaced and are currently facing severe humanitarian hardship. “Over 100 persons in various communities in Karim have been displaced and rendered homeless… exposing them to further attacks while denying them access to potable water and other basic necessities,” he said. Audu lamented that the affected communities, which are largely agrarian, have suffered significant economic losses due to the destruction of farms, crops and livestock. “The victims are suffering untold hardship as their sources of livelihood have been destroyed,” he added. Lawmakers expressed concern that recurring communal conflicts in Taraba State continue to undermine peace, security and economic activity, particularly in already vulnerable rural communities. They warned that the humanitarian situation could worsen if urgent intervention is not provided, as displaced families struggle with access to shelter, food, clean water and healthcare. The House therefore urged security agencies to intensify efforts to restore peace and prevent further escalation of the conflict. It also called on the Federal Government, the National Emergency Management Agency (NEMA) and other humanitarian bodies to provide immediate relief materials to affected communities. Lawmakers further stressed the need for a lasting resolution through dialogue, stronger security presence and sustained peace-building efforts to address the root causes of the recurring violence. The post Reps urge urgent FG action over fresh Taraba clashes, displacement appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Tinubu congratulates Jimoh Ibrahim on election as UN Budget, Administration c’ttee chair
items.description: By Johnbosco Agbakwuru President Bola Tinubu has congratulated Ambassador Jimoh Ibrahim, Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, on his election as Chairman of the United Nations General Assembly Fifth Committee on Budget and Administration. President Tinubu, in a statement by his spokesman, Bayo Onanuga, noted that the election of Senator Ibrahim is noteworthy at a particularly critical moment for the United Nations, as the world body navigates significant financial realignment while advancing critical institutional reforms to strengthen its effectiveness, accountability, and long-term sustainability. The President said: “Your election to chair this important committee within weeks of official resumption at the UN validates your wealth of experience in public service, diplomacy, business leadership, and governance, particularly as a member of the Nigerian Senate Committee on Budget and Appropriation and Chairman of Inter-Parliamentary Relations. “Your previous roles in the private sector and public service distinctly equip you to provide the steady, strategic leadership required for this important responsibility. “Through your background as a respected legal scholar with commensurate expertise in International Taxation and Business Administration, you have consistently demonstrated intellectual acumen and a deep understanding of the global governance and financial systems, providing valuable insight into fiscal management, institutional efficiency, and sustainable growth.” Tinubu said he is confident that, just as the reforms at home are yielding positive results, Ambassador Jimoh Ibrahim’s experience and unwavering commitment to excellence will justify his selection as our Permanent Representative and make Nigeria proud in the work of the Fifth Committee and in support of the broader objectives of the United Nations. “Congratulations once again on this remarkable achievement and on the confidence reposed in you by the international community,” he concluded. The post Tinubu congratulates Jimoh Ibrahim on election as UN Budget, Administration c’ttee chair appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.description: 𝙀𝙣 𝙘𝙚 𝙢𝙤𝙞𝙨 𝙙𝙚 𝙟𝙪𝙞𝙣 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲, 𝙄𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙖𝙘𝙩 𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙨𝙪𝙡𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙨𝙤𝙪𝙛𝙛𝙡𝙚 𝙨𝙚𝙨 𝙫𝙞𝙣𝙜𝙩 𝙗𝙤𝙪𝙜𝙞𝙚𝙨. Vingt années de travail, d'exigence et de convictions. Dans un secteur où peu de structures traversent le temps, atteindre ce cap n'a rien d'évident. C'est le fruit d'un engagement collectif, et c'est avec une émotion sincère que je prends la parole aujourd'hui. Mes premiers mots sont des mots de 𝗴𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲 . Merci à ma famille et à mes parents, dont le soutien a été le socle de cette aventure. Merci à nos équipes, ces femmes et ces hommes qui, jour après jour, incarnent l'excellence du cabinet. Et merci, tout particulièrement, à nos partenaires, à nos parties prenantes et surtout à nos clients. 𝗡𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝗮𝘃𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝗿𝗮𝗶𝘀𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗱 'ê 𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗳𝗶𝗲𝗿𝘀 : 𝗰𝗲 𝘀𝗼𝗻𝘁 𝗻𝗼𝘀 𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 — 𝗻𝗼𝗺𝗯𝗿𝗲𝘂𝘅 , 𝗱𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘀 , 𝗽𝗿 é 𝘀𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝗱𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝘁𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝗹𝗲𝘀 𝘀𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗲𝘂𝗿𝘀 . Pendant vingt ans, dans les mines, dans le conseil RH, juridique et fiscal, vous nous avez fait confiance. Cette confiance est notre plus belle réussite. Intellact est né à une époque où le conseil en Guinée restait fragmenté : des compétences isolées, dispersées entre quelques spécialités. Nous avons fait un pari différent — celui du 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗶𝗻𝘁 é 𝗴𝗿 é, capable d'accompagner une entreprise sur l'ensemble de ses besoins, avec des standards internationaux et une rigueur conforme aux exigences 𝗢𝗛𝗔𝗗𝗔 . Ce positionnement pionnier nous a permis de mener des missions innovantes et de satisfaire des clients aux profils variés, des grands groupes miniers aux PME locales. Mais au-delà des mandats prestigieux, une part de notre identité me rend particulièrement fier : notre 𝗲𝗻𝗴𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 . Très tôt, Intellact a placé le renforcement des capacités des jeunes au cœur de son action. Plus tard, nous avons développé des programmes dédiés aux femmes, portés par des collaborations fécondes. Parmi ces initiatives : 𝗹𝗮 𝗡𝗼𝘂𝘃𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝗜𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗚𝗮𝗴𝗻𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗲 pour l’emploi et la formation, nos 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 à 𝗰𝗼 û 𝘁 𝗺𝗼𝗱 é 𝗿 é destinées aux jeunes et aux plus démunis. Former, transmettre, ouvrir des portes : voilà une mission qui dépasse le conseil. Ces vingt années n'ont pas été un long fleuve tranquille. Nous avons affronté des défis, traversé des périodes difficiles. Si nous tenons toujours debout, c'est parce que la 𝗿 é 𝘀𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 est une vertu que nous cultivons. Chez Intellact, nous apprenons à nos collaborateurs à faire face aux obstacles, à travailler ensemble, à se donner la main. 𝗨𝗻𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗻𝗲 𝗱𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗾𝘂𝗲 𝘀𝗶 𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝗰𝗲 𝗹𝗲 𝘀𝗮𝘃𝗼𝗶𝗿 𝗮𝘂 𝗰𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗲 𝘁𝗼𝘂𝘁 . Ici, le savoir se partage, se cultive et se transmet. 𝗘𝘁 𝗰𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗲 𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗶 è 𝗺𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗻 é 𝗲 𝘀𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝘂𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲 𝗽𝗮𝗿 𝘂𝗻𝗲 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗾𝘂𝗶 𝗻𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝗵𝗼𝗻𝗼𝗿𝗲 : 𝗹𝗲 𝗣𝗿𝗶𝘅 𝗥𝗶𝗼 𝗧𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗼 2026 𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝗙𝗼𝘂𝗿𝗻𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗲𝘂𝗿𝘀 . Une reconnaissance qui valide vingt ans d'exigence et nous engage pour l'avenir. Aujourd'hui, je regarde l'avenir avec ambition. Nous voulons continuer à grandir et intégrer pleinement les nouveaux défis — à commencer par l' 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗲 , que nous inscrivons déjà au cœur de nos modes opératoires. Nous voulons amplifier notre 𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗰𝘁 𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 : l'insertion socioprofessionnelle de la jeunesse, l'émancipation des femmes, et surtout l'accompagnement des 𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗽𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗲𝘀 𝗹𝗼𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲𝘀 pour qu'elles jouent pleinement leur rôle dans le développement de notre pays. 𝗩𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝘀 , 𝗰𝗲 𝗻 ' 𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗮𝘀 𝘂𝗻𝗲 𝗳𝗶𝗻 . 𝗖 ' 𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝘂𝗻𝗲 𝗳𝗼𝗻𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 . Merci à tous ceux qui ont écrit cette histoire avec nous. Les vingt prochaines années s'écriront ensemble. 𝗜𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗵𝗶𝗺𝗮 𝗖𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗮 𝘿𝙞𝙧𝙚𝙘𝙩𝙚𝙪𝙧 𝙂𝗲́𝙣𝗲́𝙧𝙖𝙡, 𝙄𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙖𝙘𝙩 𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙨𝙪𝙡𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜

items.title: The programmer’s paradox: Why AI can’t replace humans, only the unwilling
items.description: The rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has provoked widespread debate about its implications for the future of work, creativity, ethics, and humanity itself. However, one irrefutable truth stands tall: AI is the creation of human ingenuity. This paper argues that AI cannot replace humans, not because of its limitations alone, but because it is fundamentally dependent on the human mind that programs, trains, and governs it. Rather than a wholesale replacement, AI serves as a tool to augment human capability, only replacing those who resist adaptation. With compelling global examples, expert opinions, and empirical data, this paper defends the irreplaceability of the human element in the age of intelligent machines. Introduction The debate over Artificial Intelligence replacing humans has flooded boardrooms, classrooms, and policy arenas alike. With headlines forecasting a technological apocalypse for the job market, many overlook a critical fact: AI is conceived, coded, and curated by humans. As Elon Musk put it, “AI doesn’t have a mind of its own—it’s what we feed into it.” This paper aims to reframe the conversation—not as AI versus humans, but as AI by humans and for humans. I argue that AI will only replace individuals unwilling or unable to evolve with changing technology, not those who actively adapt and lead. The Human Fingerprint on AI At its core, Artificial Intelligence is an extension of human logic and language. Every neural network, machine-learning model, and data-processing system is built on frameworks written by programmers, researchers, and engineers. According to Fei-Fei Li, co-director of the Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute, “AI reflects the values and data of the people who build it.” This inherent human authorship is what makes AI a tool rather than a threat. It cannot think or innovate independently beyond the parameters set by its creators. While machine learning allows for pattern recognition and optimization, the ethical judgment, emotional intelligence, and contextual understanding that define humanity are beyond the scope of current AI. Historical Evidence: Disruption, Not Replacement Technological revolutions are not new. The Industrial Revolution replaced some jobs but gave rise to entirely new industries. The internet did not wipe out jobs, it transformed them. In fact, a World Economic Forum (WEF) report in 2020 forecasted that while 85 million jobs may be displaced by AI by 2025, 97 million new roles may emerge that are more adapted to the division of labor between humans, machines, and algorithms. The same WEF report emphasized that skills like creativity, resilience, critical thinking, and emotional intelligence, deeply human traits, will only grow in demand. The evolution is thus not about AI replacing humans, but about humans evolving into new roles that leverage AI as a tool. Real-World Examples: People Who Adapt Win i. Healthcare Sector In radiology, AI now assists with reading X-rays faster and sometimes more accurately than humans. However, radiologists who integrate AI into their diagnostics improve patient outcomes significantly. As Dr. Eric Topol, author of “Deep Medicine” notes, “AI won’t replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace those who don’t.” ii. Journalism OpenAI’s GPT systems can write articles, but seasoned journalists who use these tools are able to draft content faster, improve headlines, and reach wider audiences. The Washington Post, for example, uses AI to automate earnings reports, freeing journalists to focus on deeper investigations. iii. Education Platforms like Khan Academy are using AI to personalize learning paths. Yet, teachers who adapt and integrate these technologies become more effective, offering individualized attention with the help of data-driven insights. It is those who refuse to adapt that risk becoming obsolete. The Cognitive Gap: Human Versus Machine AI lacks key attributes such as consciousness, morality, empathy, and creativity in the fullest sense. According to the late Stephen Hawking, “Success in creating AI would be the biggest event in human history… but it might also be the last, unless we learn how to avoid the risks.” The point here is control, humans are not only creators but also regulators of AI. Moreover, AI systems remain prone to bias, hallucinations, and data contamination, all of which require human oversight. The infamous case of Microsoft’s Tay chatbot turning racist within 24 hours online serves as a clear warning: AI is only as ethical and intelligent as the data and guidance it receives from humans. Who Gets Replaced? The Choice Factor The harsh truth is not that AI will replace humans, but that it will replace roles, not souls. Those who choose not to learn new skills, stay rigid in outdated methods, or ignore emerging tools will find themselves replaced, not by AI per se, but by humans who use AI better. The McKinsey Global Institute estimates that up to 375 million workers (14% of the global workforce) may need to switch occupations by 2030 due to automation and AI, but those transitions are made possible through reskilling and lifelong learning. In the words of Jack Ma, founder of Alibaba, “We should teach our kids to be creative and independent thinkers… machines are better at remembering, but humans are better at dreaming.” Conclusion: Programmers Cannot Be Replaced by Their Code AI is a mirror, not a mind. It reflects the intelligence and limitations of its programmers. As long as humans are the creators, curators, and conscience of AI, they remain irreplaceable. The real threat is not AI itself, but apathy towards progress. This paper affirms that AI won’t replace humans, but it will replace those who refuse to work with it.The future belongs to the human-AI hybrid workforce, where the machine enhances human potential rather than competes with it. As we navigate the AI era, adaptation, not replacement, is the key. References • World Economic Forum (2020). The Future of Jobs Report. • McKinsey Global Institute (2017). Jobs Lost, Jobs Gained: Workforce Transitions in a Time of Automation. • Li, F. (2018). Human-Centered AI. Stanford HAI. • Topol, E. (2019). Deep Medicine: How Artificial Intelligence Can Make Healthcare Human Again. • Musk, E. (Various interviews on AI, 2015–2023). • Hawking, S. (2014). BBC Interview on AI Risks. • Ma, J. (2018). World Economic Forum Annual Meeting.

items.title: Lancement des travaux du Centre de Formation Professionnelle de WINNING CONSORTIUM
items.description: Kaboyé, le 2 juin 2026- Communiqué de presse Winning Consortium a procédé hier 2 Juin 2026 à la pose de la première pierre du Centre de Formation Professionnelle de Winning Consortium à Kaboyé, dans la préfecture de Boké. Ce projet structurant s'inscrit dans la dynamique nationale de développement du capital humain et contribue à la préparation des compétences nécessaires à l'accompagnement des grandes ambitions économiques et industrielles de la Guinée, en cohérence avec les objectifs portés par le programme Simandou 2040. La cérémonie s'est tenue en présence du Dr Julien Bongono, Secrétaire Général du Ministère de l'Éducation Nationale, de l'Alphabétisation, de l'Enseignement Technique et de la Formation Professionnelle, de M. Mamadou Saliou Sylla, Secrétaire Général du Ministère de l'Urbanisme, de l'Habitat et de l'Aménagement du Territoire, de Mme Aminata Bangoura Diallo, Cheffe de Cabinet du Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie, ainsi que de Mme Gnalen Condé, Directrice de Cabinet du Gouvernorat de Boké représentant Monsieur le Gouverneur. La délégation de Winning Consortium était conduite par la Professeure Sophie Xiaohui Lv, Vice-Présidente de Winning International Group et Directrice Générale de Winning Consortium Holdings. Elle comprenait également M. Wu Qiong, Coordonnateur Général du Consortium SMB-Winning, M. Wang Zhaosheng, Directeur des Mines de la SMB, ainsi que plusieurs cadres dirigeants et responsables des différentes entités du Consortium. Les autorités locales, les notabilités, les représentants des communautés ainsi que les partenaires du projet ont également pris part à la cérémonie. Dans son intervention, Mme Gnalen Condé a salué une initiative qui contribuera au renforcement des compétences nationales et a la préparation des talents appelés à accompagner le développement économique et industriel de la Guinée. Prenant la parole au nom des employés, M. Loua Germain, Directeur de l'Exploitation de la mine de Missira, a partagé un témoignage marqué par son propre parcours au sein de SMB-Winning Consortium. Revenant sur son intégration dans l'entreprise en 2016, les formations dont il a bénéficié en Guinée et en Chine ainsi que l'accompagnement reçu tout au long de sa carrière, il a rappelé que le développement des compétences constitue avant tout une réalité vécue par de nombreux collaborateurs guinéens m dont les responsabilités n'ont cessé d'évoluer au fil des années. La Professeure Sophie Xiaohui Lv a, pour sa part, exprimé sa gratitude au Gouvernement de la République de Guinée, aux communautés locales et à l'ensemble des partenaires pour leur confiance et leur accompagnement constant. Elle a rappelé que si les infrastructures sont essentielles, leur impact repose avant tout sur les femmes et les hommes qui les conçoivent, les exploitent et les font progresser au quotidien. Évoquant la vision du Président Sun Xiushun, elle a souligné que la véritable valeur d'une entreprise ne réside pas uniquement dans ce qu'elle construit, mais également dans les compétences qu'elle contribue à développer, les talents qu'elle accompagne et les opportunités durables qu'elle laisse aux générations futures. Elle a également indiqué que ce centre traduit la volonté de Winning Consortium de contribuer durablement au développement des compétences dans les domaines minier, ferroviaire, portuaire, industriel et logistique, afin de permettre à davantage de Guinéens de saisir les opportunités créées par la transformation économique du pays. Clôturant les interventions, le Dr Julien Bongono a qualifié cette cérémonie de « première pierre d'un avenir meilleur pour des milliers de jeunes Guinéens ». Saluant l'investissement réalisé par Winning Consortium, il a souligné la convergence entre cette initiative et les ambitions nationales en matière de formation professionnelle. Il a notamment rappelé qu'aucune transformation économique durable n'est possible sans transformation du capital humain et que la véritable richesse de la Guinée réside dans sa jeunesse. Représentant un investissement de plus de 18 millions de dollars américains, le Centre de Formation Professionnelle de Winning Consortium comprendra des infrastructures pédagogiques modernes, des ateliers de formation pratique, des espaces d'hébergement, des installations sportives ainsi que des équipements destinés à répondre aux besoins des secteurs minier, ferroviaire, portuaire et industriel. A terme, il pourra accueillir jusqu'à 500 apprenants et contribuera à la formation d'une nouvelle génération de techniciens, d'opérateurs, de superviseurs et de professionnels qualifiés appelés à accompagner le développement économique et industriel de la Guinée. La cérémonie s'est achevée par le dévoilement symbolique de la première pierre autour de laquelle s'élèvera le Centre de Formation Professionnelle de Winning Consortium, marquant ainsi le lancement officiel des travaux. Ancrés dans le présent, engagés pour l'avenir.

items.title: 4 years after Owo massacre, we still nurse wounds — Bishop Arogundade
items.description: By Dayo Johnson, Akure The Catholic Bishop of Ondo Diocese, Most Rev. Jude Arogundade, says the Church continues to live with the emotional and psychological scars of the June 5, 2022 attack on St. Francis Catholic Church, Owo, four years after 41 worshippers were killed and more than 100 others injured. Speaking in an interview ahead of the fourth anniversary of the massacre, Arogundade said the Diocese is still supporting victims, survivors and families affected by the incident. “The scar has been with us since then. To this very day, we are still either caring for the children of the deceased, or still nursing the wounds of the wounded, and those who lost dear ones,” he said. He disclosed that some families remain deeply affected, including a seminarian whose parents were both killed in the attack. Reacting to the recent conviction and death sentence of four suspects, the bishop said the judgment, while meaningful, could not erase the pain of loss. “People will say at least justice was done… but it doesn’t bring back the lives of 41 people that were brutally murdered,” he said. Arogundade maintained that the Church believes the attack was planned and not random. “I still believe that it was sponsored. It is not just out of the blue. People planned it and executed it,” he said. He stressed that the Catholic Church remains focused on its humanitarian mission, noting its work in healthcare, education, and support for vulnerable groups. “We don’t get involved in controversies. We care for everybody. We have hospitals, we support orphans and widows,” he said. On forgiveness and justice, the bishop said the Church has moved on spiritually but still respects due process under the law. “We are a church. We follow the teachings of Christ… forgiveness is from God. But the law of the land must still take its course,” he added. The post 4 years after Owo massacre, we still nurse wounds — Bishop Arogundade appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Health Ministry summons KATH CEO over Emergency Centre closure
items.description: The Ministry of Health has summoned the Chief Executive at the Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital (KATH) following public statements announcing the temporary suspension of admissions at the hospital’s Accident and Emergency (A&E) Centre due to severe congestion. The move comes after the Ministry expressed concern over reports that the country’s second-largest referral hospital had halted the admission of new emergency cases for a 24-hour period, a decision which it says contradicts a standing directive issued by President John Dramani Mahama that no hospital should turn away emergency patients. In a letter issued by the Ministry on Wednesday, June 3, the CEO was directed to appear before the Minister for Health by noon on Thursday, June 4, 2026, to explain why disciplinary action should not be taken against him for the hospital management’s decision. The controversy stems from a statement released by KATH’s Public Affairs Unit indicating that the hospital’s Accident and Emergency Centre had reached critical capacity and could no longer safely admit additional patients. According to the statement, the facility’s emergency unit, originally designed to accommodate 37 patients, was operating far beyond its intended capacity. Hospital authorities disclosed that 61 patients were already occupying the Orange, Yellow and Red critical care wards, while a further 34 patients were waiting to receive medical attention. Speaking on Accra-based Channel One Television News, the Public Affairs Head reportedly reiterated the hospital’s position, explaining that the overwhelming number of patients had placed immense pressure on staff, resources and available bed space. The hospital subsequently advised the public and ambulance services to consider alternative healthcare facilities for emergency referrals during the period of congestion. However, the Ministry of Health has taken issue with the announcement, arguing that the hospital’s public position runs contrary to government policy regarding emergency healthcare delivery. READ ALSO: KATH suspends admission of new cases at Accident and Emergency Centre over congestion The Ministry stressed that President Mahama had previously issued a clear directive to all healthcare facilities across the country not to reject or turn away emergency patients under any circumstances. The Ministry subsequently directed the official to provide justification as to why disciplinary measures should not be imposed for allegedly contradicting the President’s directive.

items.title: Bamako lance le premier Forum panafricain des médias
items.description: Le premier Forum panafricain des medias (FOPAME) a officiellement ouvert ses portes ce mardi a Bamako. Organise par la Maison de la Presse du Mali en partenariat avec plusieurs acteurs du secteur, cet evenement ambitionne de renforcer la cooperation et les echanges entre les professionnels des medias africains. Pendant quatre jours, journalistes, responsables de medias, experts et acteurs de la communication venus d'une dizaine de pays africains participeront a des panels, tables rondes et sessions de reflexion sur les principaux defis du secteur. Selon les organisateurs, les discussions porteront notamment sur la transformation numerique des medias, l'intelligence artificielle, la securite des journalistes ainsi que les modeles economiques permettant d'assurer la viabilite des entreprises de presse. Des professionnels de plusieurs pays africains reunis pour reflechir a l'avenir du secteur Pour Sory Ibrahim Maïga, secretaire permanent du FOPAME, ce forum se veut avant tout un cadre de collaboration entre les medias du continent. « Les objectifs de ce forum, c'est de creer un cadre de collaboration entre les medias africains pour pouvoir creer un espace d'echange en vue de travailler sur le narratif pour que l'Afrique puisse elle-meme raconter son histoire », a-t-il explique. Il a precise que des participants issus des pays de l'AES ainsi que de plusieurs etats de la sous-region prennent part a cette rencontre. Au niveau national, pres de 250 participants sont attendus. Les travaux du forum s'acheveront par l'adoption de la « Declaration de Bamako », un document qui regroupera les principales recommandations et propositions formulees au cours des echanges afin de contribuer au developpement d'un paysage mediatique africain plus fort et mieux adapte aux mutations actuelles et contextes africains. L’article Bamako lance le premier Forum panafricain des médias est apparu en premier sur Studio Tamani - Informations, débats, magazines : toute l’actualité du Mali, en 5 langues .

items.title: Scattered rains, thunderstorms expected across Ghana from June 3 to 5 – GMet
items.description: The Ghana Meteorological Agency (GMet) has forecast mixed weather conditions across the country from June 3 to June 5, 2026, with periods of thunderstorms, rain, mist, and occasional sunny intervals expected in different ecological zones. According to the agency’s impact-based midweek forecast issued by its Central Analysis and Forecasting Office (CAFO), the overall risk level remains low, though residents are advised to stay alert to localised weather disturbances, particularly during morning and evening hours. Coastal and Inland Areas Along the coastal belt, conditions are expected to remain mostly cloudy with scattered thunderstorms and rain. Early morning mist is also likely, which may reduce visibility for motorists and commuters. Areas slightly north of the coast are expected to experience a similar pattern, beginning with early morning fog or mist patches, followed by mostly cloudy skies and isolated to scattered thunderstorms. Middle and Transition Zones The middle and transition zones are expected to record mostly cloudy conditions with scattered thunderstorms and rainfall activity. However, by Friday, some areas may experience more widespread thunderstorms, potentially affecting outdoor activities and transport operations. Northern Sector Outlook In the northern parts of the country, the forecast indicates a mix of sunny intervals and scattered thunderstorms or rain throughout the period. Early morning mist and fog patches are also expected in some locations, particularly during dawn hours.
